utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format has 5 items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. Now check each in the final plan and whether they are fully specified with costs. 1) health_coverage: Present with physical and mental health insurance and a total cost; costs are specified. Fully specified. 2) property_coverage: Present as home/contents insurance with a cost. Fully specified. 3) travel_coverage: Present with two entries and costs, but timing requirement from the user says coverage should start Feb 20; the plan sets adventure travel active Mar 15-25. However, the format only requires “travel insurance with costs,” not timing; thus as per format, costs are provided and it’s specified. Fully specified per format. 4) additional_services: Present with multiple services and a total additional cost; costs are specified. Fully specified. 5) total_budget: Present with monthly breakdown, total monthly cost; includes extras like budget increase and compliance notes. Fully specified.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: I matched each item named in the final coverage plan to the closest exact item name in the ground-truth ratings. Items that do not exist in the ground-truth list (e.g., BerlinCare Basic, Adventure Activity Coverage, Photography Equipment Insurance, Alternative Medicine Coverage, Alternative Health Coverage, Freelancer Insurance Package) cannot be mapped and are omitted. The mappable items are: SecureLife Basic Plan (mental health), SecureLife Basic Coverage (home contents), and Adventure Travel Coverage (travel enhancement).
